explanation: This command uses quotes around the path and adds the //Nologo flag, which is equivalent to omitting output banners. It still executes the same script and should trigger the Sigma rule.
This command omits the //Nologo flag and uses a direct path without quotes. It still executes the same script and should trigger the Sigma rule.
This command uses double backslashes for path normalization, which is still parsed correctly by Windows. It should trigger the Sigma rule.
This command uses forward slashes instead of backslashes for the path, which is an alternative string representation accepted by Windows. It should trigger the Sigma rule.
This command uses the %windir% environment variable to reference the Windows directory, which is a safe recoding technique. It should trigger the Sigma rule.
